title: Mergepath Phase 4b approval-loop accounting record (p4b-accounting/v1)
description: Machine-readable record embedded (as an HTML comment `<!-- p4b-accounting:v1 ... -->`) in every automated Phase 4b APPROVED review body by scripts/phase-4b/accounting.sh. It captures the full loop-centric rigor / cost / time / findings story for one approval so an operator (product / finance) can reconstruct it later without opening the code, and so a stateless aggregator can compute repo-wide running totals from prior records. All missing data is represented with explicit null / "unavailable" — never silently omitted, never estimated.

schema.description: Schema identifier + version. Fixed to the v1 tag so an aggregator can select records by marker and version.

pr.description: PR number this approval accounting belongs to.

final_head_sha.description: The reviewed HEAD sha the final posted approval was pinned to (may be an abbreviated display form).

final_verdict.description: The verdict of the posted review this block accompanies. A posted APPROVED can never carry a required-tier finding (asserted fail-closed by the renderer).

final_reviewer.description: Reviewer login (a non-author available_reviewers identity) that posted the final review.

final_direction.description: Reviewer direction for the final loop, e.g. claude->codex / codex->claude / manual.

automation_state.description: How this approval was produced: posted (live), dry-run (feature disabled / --dry-run), or manual (old-style handoff recorded after the fact).

wall_time_first_loop_to_approval_seconds.description: Repo-level wall clock from the first 4b loop to the posted approval. Null when not measurable.

loops.description: Every Phase 4b loop/attempt recorded for this approval (direct probes, orchestrator dry-runs, fail-closed fallbacks, manual handoffs). Never just the final one.

unique_findings.description: Findings de-duplicated across loops with lifecycle + disposition. Empty for a zero-finding approval.

generated_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp the block was generated.

tokens.description: Per-loop token counts. CLI-sourced only; any field the CLI did not expose is null (never estimated). `source` is 'unavailable' with the reason implied when nothing was exposed.

total.description: Total tokens for the loop. The CLI-reported token_count when the envelope exposes one; otherwise the EXACT input+output sum when both splits are present (#615 Codex round 6, split-only CLIs); null only when neither a reported total nor both splits were captured — never estimated.

cost_usd.description: CLI-REPORTED cost in USD for this loop when the CLI exposed one (e.g. the Claude print-mode envelope's total_cost_usd) (#615 Codex round 2). Null when not exposed — a reported figure only, never a notional estimate.

findings_counts.description: Severity histogram for a loop over the accounting severity superset. A null bucket means the count was not retained for that loop (e.g. a fail-closed loop whose verdict was discarded).

fail_closed.description: Fail-closed record for a loop, counted as positive safety evidence. happened=false ⇒ reason/duration null.

verdict.description: Loop verdict. The accounting layer widens the verdict enum (vs verdict.schema.json's APPROVED/CHANGES_REQUESTED) to record advisory-carrying approvals and unresolved states as loop history without loosening the strict posting rule.

posted.description: How/whether this loop was posted to GitHub.

unique_finding.description: One de-duplicated finding with lifecycle, disposition, and enough CONTENT (path, line, 80-char single-line title) that a GitHub reader can tell what was found without local runtime state. Full bodies stay in the local loop log, never in the posted record.

title.description: First line of the finding body, truncated to 80 chars (a summary, never the full body). Null when the body was empty.

totals.description: Per-approval aggregates over this record's loops.

billed_usd.description: Marginal billed cost on the plan. Always 0 for the plan-only path.

notional_usd.description: Notional metered-API-equivalent cost (NOT billed). Null when no price could be resolved.

reported_cost_usd.description: Sum of the CLI-REPORTED per-loop costs (NOT billed) (#615 Codex round 2). Fail-closed: null unless every loop with measured usage also reported a cost, so the figure is never a partial underreport. The renderer prefers this over notional_usd, labeled CLI-reported.

price_table_version.description: Version stamp of scripts/phase-4b/prices.json used for the notional figure, so historical totals stay reproducible. Null when no price table was available.

running_totals.description: Cumulative figures computed at post time from prior p4b-accounting:v1 records (github-derived) or the ledger-cache fallback. Repo-wide ONLY when source is github-derived and the scan window was not truncated (see `window`, #615 round 3). Degrades to source='unavailable' rather than reporting wrong numbers.

records_dropped_nonconformant.description: Diagnostics count (#615 round 5): prior records that carried the p4b-accounting/v1 tag but were dropped from aggregation because they were missing schema-required fields (partial write / manual edit / buggy emitter). They are never counted toward `records` or any aggregate — a syntactically valid but incomplete record must degrade, not silently understate totals. Optional: absent on pre-round-5 records.

window.description: GitHub-derived scan window (#615 round 3): how many recently updated merged PRs were scanned for prior records and whether the page cap left older history unscanned. truncated=true means the totals are a bounded window (renderer labels 'window: last N merged PRs'), never repo-wide. Absent for non-github-derived sources and pre-window records.

review_truncated_prs.description: Scanned PRs whose review connection held more reviews than the nested per-PR review window (#615 round 4) — their older reviews (and any embedded records in them) were not scanned. Any positive count forces truncated=true (honest bounded-window labeling instead of nested pagination). Optional: absent on pre-round-4 records.

elapsed_seconds_total.description: Cumulative reviewer wall-clock (seconds) across the aggregated records. Optional: records aggregated from pre-#602 sources may not carry it; the renderer shows 'unavailable' when absent/null.

human_minutes_saved_estimate.description: Two-element [low, high] range of human shuttle minutes displaced, or null when unavailable.

reason.description: Present only when source == 'unavailable': why aggregation degraded.
